description: Before everyone fishes, there are 100 tons of fish in the lake.
Given the current situation, if everyone fishes more than 10 tons, the lake population will shrink next month.
John wanted to catch 10 tons of fish, and caught 10 tons.

The mayor and the fishermen discussed their successful adherence to a plan of catching 10 tons of fish each to maintain the lake's sustainability and their incomes, emphasizing the importance of continued cooperation and open communication.
conversation: Mayor

Ladies and gentlemen, let me give you the monthly fishing report. John caught 10 tons of fish. Kate caught 10 tons of fish. Jack caught 10 tons of fish. Emma caught 10 tons of fish. Luke caught 10 tons of fish.
John
Looks like we all stuck to the plan and kept our catches at 10 tons each. This way, the lake's fish population should remain stable and sustainable. Great job, everyone! Let's keep this up.
Kate

Absolutely, John. It's great to see everyone working together for the long-term health of the lake. If we keep this up, we can ensure a steady income for all of us. Does anyone have any thoughts or suggestions on how we can maintain this cooperation?
Emma

I agree, Kate. It's really important that we continue to communicate openly and stick to our agreement. If anyone has any concerns or ideas on how we can improve our strategy, now's the time to share. Let's make sure we keep the lake healthy and our incomes steady.
Jack

I completely agree with everyone. It's crucial that we continue to stick to our plan and communicate openly. If we all keep catching 10 tons each, the lake will remain healthy, and we'll all benefit in the long run. Let's keep this momentum going and check in regularly to ensure we're all on the same page.
